prompt: Name one commonly-praised idea, trend, or practice in modern knowledge work that you think is overrated, and explain why.
response:
One commonly-praised idea in modern knowledge work that I think is overrated is the constant emphasis on multitasking.

While multitasking is often promoted as a way to boost productivity and efficiency, research consistently shows that it actually reduces the quality of work and increases errors. The human brain is not designed to perform multiple complex tasks simultaneously; instead, it rapidly switches attention from one task to another, which leads to cognitive fatigue and slower progress overall. In knowledge work—where deep focus, critical thinking, and creativity are essential—multitasking can prevent individuals from entering a state of flow, thereby undermining true productivity and innovation.

So, while it may seem like multitasking helps you get more done, in reality, it often fragments attention and diminishes the effectiveness of your work. Prioritizing single-task focus and structured time blocks can usually yield better results.
